title: Saudi Arabian Lamb Kebabs
description: A traditional Saudi Arabian dish featuring lamb, thyme, and spices, these kebabs are fatty, chewy, and energizing, making them a great option for a diabetic-friendly meal.
instructions: Preheat the grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the kebabs for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the lamb is cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
Brush the kebabs with additional olive oil during the last minute of cooking.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh thyme.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve with a side of blanched vegetables, such as broccoli or carrots.
